title: Sport action in full swing at WeBuyCars Tens
description: GEORGE NEWS - The WeBuyCars George Tens...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The WeBuyCars George Tens Sport and Lifestyle Festival is underway at Outeniqua Park, offering an exciting blend of sports and entertainment for the whole family.

Now in its fifth year, the festival has become a highlight on George's calendar.


> Initially launched as a rugby-focused event, it has expanded to include netball, dodgeball, a colour run, tug-of-war, air-rifle shooting and much more.


Visitors can also enjoy live music performances, food stalls and cultural activities. There is something for everyone!


> The event runs until 19 December.


In the women’s rugby, Tigers are on the attack against Motherwell. Photo: Jacques du Toit

MUST - The Ball Busters (George-Suid Primêr mixed team) on the attack against Stars Academy from Mossel Bay. Photo: Jacques du Toit

Knysna Primary on the attack against Laurus Primary. Photo: Jacques du Toit

Pretty in pink - the Kraaifontein Warriors from Cape Town. Photo: Jacques du Toit

Players of Motherwell Jaguars from Port Elizabeth warm up before their next match. Photo: Jacques du Toit

Sprinting for his try is Sean Veale of Hibernia Primary in their u.13 match against False Bay. Photo: Jacques du Toit

Warming up before their next match are players from the Amazonians Rugby Academy in Cape Town. Photo: Jacques du Toit

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
